findings.problem: Once the compensation clause lands, the chapter runs ten consecutive paragraphs of crowd reaction without Halima's interior filter. That turns a high-stakes escalation into a briefly external-only sequence and violates the requirement to keep the focalizer's inner experience present over longer paragraph runs.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:91 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:109, keep the shouted claims but braid in Halima's bodily or interpretive processing before the scene moves to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:111. Let at least one voice hit a specific nerve in her memory or professional knowledge, and let the panic register through her body or judgment instead of reading as a pure chorus.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:91 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:109 contains at least two paragraphs of explicit Halima interiority (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment) amid the crowd outburst, so there is no 10-paragraph run in that stretch without her inner experience. Fail if the sequence remains purely external shouting across those cited lines.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same spatial-pressure construction often enough to become a tic: the yard or room carries the emotional action instead of Halima's body or the crowd's concrete movement. After the first instance, the repetition starts to feel patterned rather than freshly observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the pressure beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:273. Keep only the strongest one or two spatial-compression sentences; recast the others through specific bodily consequence, crowd behavior, or object/sound change so each beat lands differently.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:191, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:195, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:273, no more than two pressure beats still use place-noun subjects such as yard, schoolyard, or room to carry emotional force, and at least two are rewritten to land through Halima's body or a concrete crowd/object action. Fail if three or more still rely on the same spatial-reaction shortcut.

summary: Strong causal opening chapter: the evacuation order lands inside a live birth, the compensation rule becomes legible immediately, Aminul correctly identifies Halima as the island's necessary witness, and the oilcloth-ledger hook closes the chapter in line with the boundary pack. Halima, Nura, Aminul, and Mofiz all track their style-bible registers, dialogue stays natural and contraction-appropriate, the narration remains in canonical past tense, and I found no continuity contradictions, example-line reuse, sanitization drift, timidity, or intimacy fade-to-black. The chapter still fails craft and prose on two MEDIUM issues: the first schoolyard panic drops Halima's interiority for ten straight paragraphs, and the prose repeats a spatial-pressure sentence formula enough times to read as a tic.
